title: A pathology foundation model for cancer diagnosis and prognosis prediction
methods: multiple instance learning (MIL) with attention aggregation
HoverNet cell segmentation for microenvironment analysis
data_sources: Institutional slides: DFCI, BWH, YH, SMCH, CUCH, UPenn
core_conclusion: CHIEF, a general-purpose weakly supervised pathology foundation model pre-trained on 60,530 WSIs (44 TB) spanning 19 anatomical sites, outperforms specialized state-of-the-art deep learning models by up to 36.1% across cancer detection, tumour origin identification, molecular profiling, and survival prediction, with robust validation in 19,491 WSIs from 32 independent international cohorts across 24 hospitals.
why_it_matters: Breaks the one-model-per-task bottleneck in pathology AI; addresses the domain-shift generalization crisis across hospitals and slide protocols; bridges imaging and genomics by predicting mutations from H&E alone; enables survival prognosis in resource-limited settings; fully open-source for global adoption.
why_nature: Universal scope spanning 19 anatomical sites and 32 independent international cohorts; architecturally novel two-stage complementary pre-training; direct societal impact on global cancer diagnosis; rigorous validation standard meeting Nature's clinical-readiness benchmark.
added_value_to_my_research: Spatial context tokens in CHIEF pre-training -> geospatially-conditioned pathology model for health disparity research using 2SFCA access scores
Causal biomarker modeling of CHIEF attention -> analogue for separating place effects from data confounders in GeoAI spatial prediction
CHIEF two-stage pre-training recipe (unsupervised tiles + weakly supervised slides) -> blueprint for multi-source geospatial data fusion with RS and POI data
Concurrent whole-slide model benchmarking -> design principles for pre-training strategy selection in geospatial foundation model fine-tuning
CHIEF survival embeddings + R2SFCA access scores -> spatially-resolved cancer prognosis map separating biology risk from access-barrier risk

CHIEF outperformed state-of-the-art deep learning methods in detecting cancer cells using WSIs across multiple cancer types
CHIEF successfully predicted genetic mutations across cancer types using histopathology images
CHIEF predicted IDH status of glioma samples and MSI status of patients with CRC in several independent cohorts
CHIEF predicted survival outcomes of patients with cancer, validated in 15 validation cohorts collected from hospitals worldwide
